preamble: The elision of the labio-velar approximant [w] is a common sound change, particularly in complex clusters or word-final positions.

examples.note: Loss of the initial [w] in [wr] clusters during the Early Modern English period.
Systematic loss of the historical [w] sound (digamma) across most Ancient Greek dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
